quotes.verbatim_quote:
The following four chords: Minor Triad, Minor Sixth Chord, Minor Seventh Chord and Minor Ninth
Chord will be formed by applying a numerical formula to the B Dorian Mode. The corresponding
alphabetical symbols will also be indicated.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This passage establishes the method's core approach: all minor chord types are derived from a single Dorian Mode via numerical formulas, unifying the chapter's technique under one system.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
By extracting the tones No. !-3-5 from the B Dorian Mode, we arrive at the notes forming the B
Minor Triad.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Defines the foundational minor triad construction rule from which all subsequent minor chord extensions are built.

quotes.verbatim_quote: Tones No. 1-3-5-6 of the B Dorian Mode form the B Minor Sixth Chord.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Gives the precise numerical formula that distinguishes the minor sixth chord from the basic triad, teaching the extension pattern.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
The B Dorian Mode is illustrated in two octaves. The numbers under each cone indicate the position
of that tone in this scale. Note that beyond the first octave, tone No. 8, the tones have the same
letter names and the numbers corresponding to these tones refer to the respective distances from the
starting tone, No. 1.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Explains how the book's numbering system extends across octaves, which is essential for correctly interpreting any formula that includes tones above 7.
